title: On An Old Gate. Erected In Chiswick Gardens.
content:
O gate, how cam'st thou here?
    Gate. I was brought from Chelsea last year,
    Batter'd with wind and weather.
    Inigo Jones put me together;
    Sir Hans Sloane
    Let me alone:
    Burlington brought me hither.
meaning:
Alexander Pope's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On An Old Gate. Erected In Chiswick Gardens."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
